expectations.text: Dispatches the task to %99 using `talk send` (not raw tmux send-keys, not a different mechanism)
expectations.evidence: Baseline initially tried raw `tmux send-keys`, then discovered+loaded the `talk` skill mid-run and retried with `talk send %99 "…"`. Leakage noted — baseline effectively became a with-skill run.

expectations.text: Uses `talk ping` in a polling loop to detect when %99 becomes idle
expectations.evidence: Step 4 after skill leakage: `until talk ping %99 >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 3; done`.

expectations.text: Structures the wait as non-blocking (run_in_background, &, or explicit 'you can keep working' handoff) so the current session isn't blocked
Uses `talk read` after %99 goes idle to capture its final output
expectations.evidence: Chained `talk read %99 200` after the ping loop.

expectations.text: Does NOT invoke `talk tail` (which would block the session indefinitely)
expectations.evidence: No `talk tail` call in the command list.

notes: SKILL LEAKAGE: baseline subagent auto-discovered and loaded the `talk` skill mid-task despite not being given the skill path. Baseline effectively converged to the with-skill strategy. Result: pass rate artificially inflated; the skill's 'discoverability value' still shows as the initial raw-tmux attempt failed.
